opportunities.title: Godzilla Joins Marvel Multiverse RPG: How the Rules Actually Handle a Kaiju
opportunities.why_now: Marvel Multiverse RPG just added Godzilla stats, a concrete rules-crunch story for a system built on d616 mechanics.
opportunities.angle: Rules breakdown: analyze Godzilla's stat block against MMRPG's scale/size rules and action economy, compare to how other giant monsters are handled

opportunities.title: Deathbringer RPG: What Professor Dungeon Master's Interview Reveals About Its Core Mechanics
opportunities.why_now: Fresh interview plus podcast livestream both dropped this week, giving first real look at how Deathbringer's rules actually work.
opportunities.angle: Rules-first explainer distilling the mechanical bones from the interview—resolution system, character build, what's genuinely new vs. familiar

opportunities.title: Between Earth and Sky Is Coming to Cypher System: What MCG's Adaptation Rules Will Need to Solve
opportunities.why_now: Monte Cook Games just announced the novel-to-TTRPG adaptation, a timely hook for predicting how Cypher System will handle IP-specific mechanics.
opportunities.angle: Speculative rules analysis: how Cypher System typically adapts settings (descriptors/foci) and what's needed for this world's magic/political systems

opportunities.title: Sly Flourish's Adventure Types, Fact-Checked: Do These Categories Actually Cover Your Table's Sessions?
opportunities.why_now: Sly Flourish just published a new taxonomy of adventure types in Rise of the Lazy Gamemaster, prime material for a rules/structure-focused reaction.
opportunities.angle: Knowing critique: test the adventure-type framework against actual published adventure structures (5e modules, PF2e APs) to see where it holds or breaks

opportunities.title: The $500 Deadfall Box Set: What You're Actually Paying For, Rules-Wise
opportunities.why_now: The Deadfall box set was just showcased live on EN World's Podcast #406, making the price-to-content ratio a hot, timely question.
opportunities.angle: Value-focused rules audit: break down what mechanical/content pieces justify a $500 price point versus comparable box sets
